meta.title: Video by jinabaobina
meta.description:
I would fly back to New York just for this

I already love Chinese food, but this spot made me love it even more. We ordered so much off the menu, and honestly… I’d get almost everything again. Next time, I’d probably reorder all of these and just add new dishes on top.

I wish I had more footage because this meal deserved a longer video, but here’s everything I need to say about my favorites:

📍 Antidote in Brooklyn (I think they also have a second location) @antidote__nyc 

Scallion pancake $12: So flaky and perfectly crisp

Dan dan noodles $14: Chewy noodles with the perfect balance of spice and sesame

Preserved egg with tofu $15: I was hesitant at first, but this ended up being one of my favorites. The flavor and spice were so good.

Sliced pork with garlic sauce $17: I can’t believe I didn’t include this in the video… this was actually my favorite dish 

Sichuan cold noodles $12: Super flavorful with that rich peanut sauce

I miss this place so much. I wasn’t planning a trip back to New York anytime soon… but honestly, I would go back just for Antidote.

#newyorkfood #chinesefood #brooklyn #brooklynfood #newyork

transcript.transcript: This restaurant in New York was so good I forgot to keep filming once I started eating. It's located in Brooklyn. My friends who live here told me it's one of their favorite spots for Chinese food so I had to try it and they were right. This ended up being my favorite place I ate during my entire trip. The only downside is now that I'm back in LA I can't have this whenever I want. We ordered so much and honestly I'd get everything again. My favorites were the Dandan noodles, preserved egg with tofu, Sichuan cold noodles, wontons and chili oil, and the scallion pancake. The vibe is so nice and the prices are very fair. I genuinely hope more people get to try this place because it made me love Chinese food even more than I already did. Would you try antidote?
